meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for AI Builders: Solving Collaboration
content.seo_description: Stop working in isolation. Learn how to master project management for AI apps using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your student portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks in AI development usually stem from fragmented context. By integrating structured project management with Claude Code and Antigravity, you unify your codebase, documentation, and agentic workflows. This creates a 'single source of truth' that allows you to manage complex tasks while keeping your AI development pipeline moving efficiently.
content.intro:
The screen glows at 2:00 AM. You’ve got a brilliant AI app concept, but your codebase is a chaotic tangle of half-finished features and broken dependencies. You are hitting the classic wall: the inability to collaborate or scale your logic because your project lacks a backbone. You’re not just writing code; you’re building a digital entity, and without structure, that entity will eventually collapse under the weight of its own complexity.

But here is the secret: professional engineering isn't about raw coding speed—it’s about systematic management. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for rapid execution, you transform your workflow from a lonely struggle into a professional-grade development cycle. It’s time to move beyond the 'solo hobbyist' phase and start building with the precision of a senior lead.

steps.title: Define Project Scope with a PRD
steps.description: Before writing code, create a Product Requirement Document (PRD) to define constraints. This prevents scope creep and keeps your AI agent focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project goals and generate a structured PRD including core features, technical constraints, and a roadmap for the first three milestones.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a PRD.md file based on the project requirements defined in the root directory.'

steps.title: Establish a Task Tracking Schema
steps.description: Implement a lightweight task system within your repo. This allows Claude Code to understand what needs to be done next without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tasks.json schema that tracks feature progress, priority levels, and assigned AI agent roles for the current sprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize tasks.json with a backlog of 5 critical tasks for the MVP.'

steps.title: Automate Context Injection
steps.description: Claude Code needs to know the project history to collaborate effectively. Use a context-injection script to update the agent on recent changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a python script that summarizes recent git commits and appends them to a context.txt file for LLM ingestion.

steps.title: Modularize the Codebase Architecture
steps.description: Break your app into isolated modules. This makes it easier to assign specific tasks to AI agents without triggering global dependency errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current monolithic structure into clean, modular components with clear interfaces for inter-module communication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Move backend logic into a /services directory and implement modular exports.'

steps.title: Execute Sprint via Claude Code
steps.description: Use your structured tasks to execute a sprint. Claude Code will now act as a pair programmer, pulling from your PRD and task list.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current task list and provide a step-by-step execution plan for the next feature implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute next task from tasks.json and update the status to completed upon success.'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'Decision Log' file; AI agents perform better when they can reference why a architectural choice was made months ago.
Use semantic versioning for your internal APIs, even if it's just a student project, to track breaking changes automatically.
Treat your README as a live document; have Claude Code update it every time you complete a major milestone to keep your documentation in sync.
Implement 'Dry Runs' by asking Claude Code to output a plan before executing any file-system changes.
Keep your context window clean by archiving old PRDs; excessive historical data can lead to hallucinated requirements.
content.next_steps: Once your project management system is live, deploy your MVP to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and share your project link in our Discord channel for peer code reviews.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to build professional-grade AI applications that scale. Don't let your ideas remain as folders of scattered scripts—apply this structure, master these tools, and ship your product. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
